title: Succession

characters.name: Logan Roy
characters.archetype: The patriarch. Founder. Refuses to die. The whole show orbits him.

characters.name: Kendall Roy
characters.archetype: The chosen heir who was never quite chosen. Wants it desperately. Falls apart in public.

characters.name: Shiv Roy
characters.archetype: The only daughter. Smartest. Quietly the most ruthless. Keeps getting passed over.

characters.name: Roman Roy
characters.archetype: The youngest, the funniest, the most damaged. Says the cruelest things while crying.

characters.name: Connor Roy
characters.archetype: The eldest. Half-brother. Thinks he's running for President. Mostly forgotten.

characters.name: Tom Wambsgans
characters.archetype: Shiv's husband. Operator. Survivor. Boar on the floor. Eventually wins everything.

characters.name: Greg Hirsch
characters.archetype: The cousin nobody wanted. Falls upward through pure incompetence and proximity.

characters.name: Gerri Kellman
characters.archetype: Waystar's general counsel. Roman's complicated almost-something. The actual adult in the room.

signature_quotes: I love you, but you are not serious people.
You can't make a Tomlette without breaking some Greggs.
I don't even want to be CEO of Pierce, I want to be CEO of Sweden!
scenes: Logan dying on the plane in S4E3.
Kendall's '90s rap performance for Logan's birthday.
Boar on the floor — the dinner of psychological torture.
The election night episode — the kids decide an election.
off_screen_drama: Jeremy Strong's method acting is its own subplot — the New Yorker profile.
Brian Cox is famously not impressed with method acting.
Cousin Greg's tower of comedy bits.
